note: Maintained log. lastUpdate = observed current state of each live app (read Jul 22). nextSteps = my read of visible gaps — Jack to confirm/replace with real roadmap.

projects.name: FADE
projects.lastUpdate: Close to launch-ready (formerly GhostBet). This weekend's customization work shipped: Team leagues, Reactivate flow, two-step Create wizard with inline explanations, Past Leagues tab, public signup, cleaner match/prop-only market-cap selector, Survivor removed. Publicly signup-able at /signup — ready for test users. BLOCKED externally: D-U-N-S case #34667209 is 8 business days overdue with no word from D&B (escalation comment posted; Apple Dev Support next if no movement by Wed EOD). No D-U-N-S = iOS TestFlight / App Store blocked, so native mobile may slip past the Aug 16 EPL opener.
nextSteps: Wait 24h on the D&B comment before escalating to Apple.
Enable email confirmation in Supabase Auth (5-min task; unblocks password reset for invited users).
Kick off the Capacitor Android wrapper — Android is unblocked, so Google Play can ship while iOS waits.
Add content moderation on avatar uploads before public launch.

projects.name: LAFC Analytics
projects.lastUpdate: Dashboard restored and live on Vercel on a fresh Supabase project (lafc-analytics): all 16 LAFC matches loaded, RLS locked read-only, season-stats view + starter RPC rebuilt, scraper fixed (headful to beat WhoScored's anti-bot). In flight: backfilling the full MLS season (~207 matches, ~6–7h, resumable) to build the percentile pool the player-fingerprint feature needs.
nextSteps: Confirm the morning summary shows the percentile pool loaded.
Build the fingerprint layer on top — percentile profiles, DNA composites, and the Scout ranker.
Rebuild the match-report Edge Function so the Generate Report button works again.

projects.name: Public portfolio
projects.lastUpdate: Live editorial layout: dark Tzolis hero anchoring a one-screen home, four Substack articles, the Yumpu presentation, three LinkedIn posts, and the heatmap wired into the dossier header. Architecture proven — the notebook and portfolio share one database, so a report edited in the terminal flows to the public page automatically (latest Tzolis edit is in the DB, awaiting a cache refresh).
nextSteps: Force a redeploy to push the pending Tzolis text edit live; decide whether to shorten the revalidation window so future notebook edits appear within minutes.
Port the scouting terminal's real report layout onto the dossier page so it matches the notebook (still rendering the earlier invented format).

projects.name: Public scouting notebook — ScoutDraft
projects.lastUpdate: Working, open-signup app: full 2,612-player pool from Sportmonks with trustworthy within-position percentiles, plus a stats panel, radar toggle, filtering, Compare and shortlists — all browser-verified. The Galaxy feature was deliberately cut; the Transfermarkt market-value idea is parked (no legal way to source it).
nextSteps: Run the 12-step test pass and send back what to change (becomes the next build prompt).
UX friction cleanup, then a colors pass.
Wire Resend SMTP so password reset works.

projects.name: Personal scouting notebook — SCOUTING_TERMINAL
projects.lastUpdate: Live with a full module suite — Players, Teams, Match Notes, Compare, Tactical Board, Shortlists and a new STATS tab — on a cream/editorial aesthetic (Playfair Display, Space Mono, Inter). World Cup 2026 transcription workflow active with 14+ matches logged (group stage + Round of 32). The JB Report PDF generates clean single-page player reports with heatmaps, color-coded category cards and correct typography. Tzolis has a complete onboarding report from 3 all-action watches — heatmap uploaded, archetypes assigned — but his radar scores and Stats API ID are still pending.
nextSteps: Run the em-dash SQL cleanup on Tzolis' DB fields.
Build Tzolis' radar scores from his notes.
Keep logging World Cup matches as you watch them.
Run the queued URL-overflow + STATS-tab + card-text-size prompt.

projects.name: FanSlate
projects.lastUpdate: 'Your American teams, your English club' — pick across NFL/NBA/MLB/NHL/CFB/CBB and 'Reveal my club' matches you to an English side. Just generated all 3,600 grounded per-team blurbs (cached, passing automated checks — no em-dashes, no length outliers, no dupes), replacing the old thin, repetitive copy with specific, fact-grounded lines for every team-club pair. Remaining gate is human: a banned-phrase sweep across the corpus and a voice read of a sample.
nextSteps: Commit the work — 3,600 blurbs and the API spend are uncommitted right now.
Confirm the result page reads from blurbs.json (old template as fallback), then push and deploy.
Then: mobile polish and the socials launch content.
